name: Israel Discount Bank Of New York
description_short: Israel Discount Bank of New York is the largest Israeli-owned bank operating in the U.S., offering commercial banking, private banking, and wealth management services to businesses and high-net-worth individuals.
description_long:
Israel Discount Bank of New York (IDB Bank) is a full-service commercial and private bank with deep roots in Israeli ownership and international banking expertise. The bank positions itself as the largest Israeli-owned bank in the United States and emphasizes a relationship-driven, personalized approach to banking that distinguishes it from larger institutional competitors.

IDB Bank offers a comprehensive suite of banking services across multiple sectors. Their commercial banking division provides industry-specific expertise in healthcare banking, commercial real estate, high-tech, food and beverage, apparel and consumer products, and non-profit sectors. The bank offers commercial lending with loan syndication capabilities, treasury services including cash management and foreign exchange, and merchant services. On the private banking side, they provide domestic and international private banking solutions, wealth management for high-net-worth clients, and personalized financial strategies. Additional services include trade finance, asset-based lending, and self-directed investment/brokerage options.

The bank distinguishes itself through sector-specific expertise and a 24/7 team availability model. They emphasize innovation alongside traditional banking relationships, claiming to offer "a new chapter rooted in relationships" while bringing "an innovative mindset" to banking. Recent news highlights include appointing a President of Commercial Banking and arranging $103 million in senior debt financing for skilled nursing facilities, demonstrating active commercial lending operations.

However, as a specialized bank focused on commercial and private clients rather than retail consumers, IDB Bank is not positioned for general consumer banking needs. Their services cater primarily to business owners, high-net-worth individuals, and established commercial enterprises rather than typical retail checking/savings customers. Limited information is available on their specific lending rates, fees, or account minimums from the website. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Commercial banking and lending with loan syndication
Commercial real estate lending and advisory
Treasury services including cash management and foreign exchange
Industry-specific expertise in high-tech, food & beverage, apparel, and non-profit sectors
pros: Specializes in industry-specific commercial lending with expert teams in healthcare, real estate, high-tech, and food & beverage sectors
Offers international private banking and treasury services including foreign exchange and trade finance
Provides 24/7 team support and personalized relationship-based banking approach
Demonstrates active commercial lending capacity with recent $103M financing arrangement for skilled nursing facilities
Full suite of commercial solutions including asset-based lending, loan syndication, and merchant services
Operates as largest Israeli-owned bank in U.S., indicating established market presence and regulatory standing
Offers wealth management and tailored financial strategies for high-net-worth clients
cons: Not positioned for retail consumer banking—no information on basic checking/savings accounts for individual consumers
Requires business establishment or high-net-worth status; minimum account requirements likely substantial but not disclosed
Limited transparency on website regarding specific lending rates, fees, account minimums, or service costs
International focus suggests higher complexity and potential higher costs for standard banking needs
No online account opening or digital banking information readily apparent on website

contact.address: 1114 Avenue Of The Americas, New York, NY 10036

best_for: Small to mid-sized business owners in healthcare, commercial real estate, technology, or specialized industries
High-net-worth individuals seeking private banking, wealth management, and international financial services
Companies requiring commercial lending with industry-specific expertise and relationship-based banking support
Businesses needing treasury services, foreign exchange, or trade finance capabilities
diagnosis: IDB Bank is best suited for established business owners and high-net-worth individuals seeking specialized commercial lending, private banking, and wealth management services, particularly those in targeted industries like healthcare and technology. The primary caveat is that this is not a retail consumer bank—individuals seeking basic checking, savings, or personal loans should look elsewhere.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 1114 Avenue Of The Americas, New York, NY 10036
